title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Chippy Quinn interrogates Miss Mulgrew to reconstruct the events surrounding the first murder of Letty Blacklock. Quinn proposes a theory regarding the killer's method: the perpetrator likely oiled a door to ensure a silent exit and used a distraction involving a flashlight and a shouted "robbery" at the front door to mask their movements. During this distraction, Quinn suggests the killer slipped through a second door, shot Letty Blacklock twice, shot a Swiss man, and dropped the gun to frame the victim.

To establish the movements of the guests when the lights failed, Quinn notes that Patrick Simmons was in the living room, Philippa Hemmes was in the small parlor, and Quinn himself was by the fireplace. He argues that because Miss Mulgrew was positioned behind a door, she was not blinded by the flashlight like the others, making her the only person capable of seeing anyone who was not officially accounted for. Quinn lists several people present, including Dora Bunner and Mrs. Harmon, and urges Miss Mulgrew to identify anyone missing from the scene.

The interrogation is interrupted by a telephone call from the police station informing Quinn that a red setter has been left at the station without water since eight o'clock that morning. Outraged by this negligence, Quinn storms out to his car to drive to the station. As he speeds away, Miss Mulgrew calls out that "Jane wasn't there," but Quinn does not hear her. Miss Mulgrew is left alone as it begins to rain.
